author: pietroGru
body:
#### PR description:
The PR addresses the crash reported in [cmssw/issues/50587](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/issues/50587).

From deeper investigation, it results the cause is a channel containing [UNORDERED_DATA](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/CMSSW_16_0_4/RecoLocalTracker/SiStripClusterizer/plugins/alpaka/SiStripRawToClusterAlgo.dev.cc#L50-L54). The block of digis which corresponds to this channel are not unpacked. This causes undefined behaviour as the strip digi collection is not initialized.

There are two possible outcomes, depending on the digi values spanning the not-unpacked fed channel:
(a) the [channel index](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/CMSSW_16_0_4/RecoLocalTracker/SiStripClusterizer/plugins/alpaka/SiStripRawToClusterAlgo.dev.cc#L368) is within the bounds of [mapping](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/CMSSW_16_0_4/RecoLocalTracker/SiStripClusterizer/plugins/alpaka/SiStripRawToClusterAlgo.dev.cc#L369). The seeding step generates cluster candidates and clusterization occurs. The cluster candidate is marked valid and the SoA-legacy conversion fails because another 'legit' cluster shares the detId (i.e., which is stored [here](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/CMSSW_16_0_4/RecoLocalTracker/SiStripClusterizer/plugins/alpaka/SiStripRawToClusterAlgo.dev.cc#L558)). This is observed the exception reported in [cmssw/issues/50587](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/issues/50587).

(b) the channel index is larger than mapping bounds. A fatal exception is raised when trying to access the SoA out of range.

Zero initialization of the strip digi collection is sufficient to ensure that channels which are not unpacked (in general, those returning non zero unpacking status code) do not generate cluster candidates.

Investigating the issue I realized two spots for further improvements:
(a) removing any runtime checks for isDebug - leftover of debugging messages
(b) reserve the legacy cluster collection in the SoA-legacy converter


#### PR validation:
Tested on the [reproducer](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/issues/50587#issue-4169541880) on run 402512 with no crashes.

Timing checked on the timing server with run 402360 and by running tracking only
- baseline [554.1  0.8 evt/s](https://timing-gui-tsg-steam.app.cern.ch/display/pgrutta/CMSSW_16_0_4_ReducedIterativeTracking_baseline.20260409_001146)
- with the proposed fix [557.6  0.5 evt/s](https://timing-gui-tsg-steam.app.cern.ch/display/pgrutta/CMSSW_16_0_4_ReducedIterativeTracking_target_v2.20260409_001133)
showing no degradation in performance
title: Protection for DetSetVector::inserv exception in sistrip::SiStripClustersToLegacy
